---
title: "Does Win XP hibernation interfere with disk mounting?"
date: 2007-05-24
forum: General Help
---

### Post by cnayak on 2007-05-24
I installed ntfs-3g sometime ago and mounted the two (one ntfs and the other fat 32) Windows partition. I've noticed that whenever, I hibernate in Win XP, the C: doesn't mount. Is that a shortcoming of ntfs-3g or have I incorrectly configured the fstab? Heres the fstab configuration:

```

# /etc/fstab: static file system information.
#
#  -- This file has been automaticly generated by ntfs-config --
#
# <file system> <mount point>   <type>  <options>       <dump>  <pass>

proc /proc proc defaults 0 0
# Entry for /dev/sda4 :
UUID=30239521-236f-4fa3-9880-b096945733d2 / ext3 defaults,errors=remount-ro 0 1
# Entry for /dev/sda1 :
UUID=DCA3-11EF /media/sda1 vfat defaults,utf8,umask=007,gid=46 0 1
# Entry for /dev/sda2 :
UUID=3CE08393E08351D6 /media/sda2 ntfs-3g defaults,locale=en_IN 0 1
# Entry for /dev/sda3 :
UUID=a439c020-97fb-4e6c-9962-26f51fd8ad3a none swap sw 0 0
/dev/scd0 /media/cdrom0 udf,iso9660 user,noauto 0 0
```

---

### Post by kidders on 2007-05-25
Hi there,

Your system logs should tell you for sure, but it seems reasonable to suggest that XP might not flag its system disk as clean until it actually shuts down. That might prevent Linux mounting it without being forced ... which is something I wouldn't recommend ... it's conceivable that modifying anything Windows had open at the time it was hibernated could cause odd behaviour or make it crash, by creating paradoxes.

[SIZE=1][COLOR=Silver][*[UAResolved]*]("http://ubuntuforums.org/showthread.php?t=377083")[/COLOR][/SIZE]
